description: Alerting rules for the GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain scenario (Cloud Audit)

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP IAM PrivEsc Chain — Service Account Key Creation
relatedDashboards: GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
GCP Cloud Audit Logs — API Activity
rules.investigationGuide:
# CreateServiceAccountKey (Non-Admin)

A non-admin created a new service account key.

## Triage
1. Check caller vs target: `FROM logs-gcp.audit-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action == "google.iam.admin.v1.CreateServiceAccountKey" | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, gcp.audit.request, source.ip, user_agent.original | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. If caller != target service account, high signal
3. Is target service account privileged?

## Escalate when
- Caller != target principal
- Target is a production service account
- Source IP outside corp/VPN

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP IAM PrivEsc Chain — SetIamPolicy
relatedDashboards: GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
GCP Cloud Audit Logs — API Activity
rules.investigationGuide:
# roles/owner Granted via SetIamPolicy

A non-admin granted roles/owner (or broad IAM) via SetIamPolicy — very high signal.

## Triage
1. Confirm: `FROM logs-gcp.audit-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action == "SetIamPolicy" AND gcp.audit.request LIKE "*roles/owner*" | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, gcp.audit.request, source.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. Revoke the binding immediately
3. Check caller's full activity timeline in Cloud Audit Logs

## Escalate when
- **Always page security on-call.** Owner-grant by non-admin is rare and high-confidence.

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP IAM PrivEsc Chain — GenerateAccessToken
relatedDashboards: GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
GCP Cloud Audit Logs — API Activity
rules.investigationGuide:
# GenerateAccessToken With Privilege-Escalation Tactic

Access-token generation (or related impersonation such as SignJwt) was tagged as privilege escalation.

## Triage
1. Check tactic: `FROM logs-gcp.audit-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action == "google.iam.credentials.v1.GenerateAccessToken" AND threat.tactic.name IS NOT NULL | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, gcp.audit.request, threat.tactic.name, source.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. Trace what the principal did next
3. Revoke the session or binding if suspicious

## Escalate when
- Principal has admin or production access
- Multiple GenerateAccessToken or SignJwt events from same caller

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP IAM PrivEsc Chain — High Volume of Sensitive IAM API Calls
relatedDashboards: GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
GCP Cloud Audit Logs — API Activity
rules.investigationGuide:
# High Volume of Dangerous IAM Actions

**Apex rule — treat as confirmed privilege escalation.**

## Triage
1. Identify principal: `FROM logs-gcp.audit-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action IN ("google.iam.admin.v1.CreateServiceAccountKey", "SetIamPolicy", "google.iam.credentials.v1.GenerateAccessToken") | STATS dangerous = COUNT(*) BY user.name, event.action | SORT dangerous DESC`
2. Reconstruct full timeline on the linked dashboard
3. Disable the principal, rotate keys, revoke IAM bindings

## Escalate when
- **Always.** This is the apex rule of the IAM PrivEsc chain.
